behavior_summary: Implements the gameplay for a thrown dart item: selecting/receiving a target, validating a candidate placement/throw origin, running short pre-throw animations, pushing the dart into the world for timing, applying dexterity-based inaccuracy, and finally launching the projectile via Item::shoot.
Provides a minimal U8P_DART::look handler that displays the short name 'dart' when examined.

Handles U8P_DART::hit/impact reactions: plays SFX, clears references, issues context barks for NPC targets, triggers enterFastArea on U8P_DART::hit NPCs, and supports a special-type bull's-eye branch that sets item status and awards dexterity.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DART::use() suspends to capture a target selection into local1, then reads target and thrower coordinates and computes direction to target.
Placement is gated by canExistAt and Item::legal_move; the throw sequence proceeds only if placement is legal.

Throw sequence spawns an animation (spawn -> METHOD::func091C), sets item frame, calls internal timing helper U8P_DART::func046A, pushes the item, waits on spawned FREE delays, then pops and spawns SFX/effects before shooting.
Accuracy is computed as an inaccuracy range = 0x1E - Npc::getDex(thrower). If > 1, random offsets (urandom) are applied to X/Y/Z before Item::shoot.

U8P_DART::hit(uword, word) contains branching: NPC-specific reaction when param is an NPC and alive; separate special-type (Item::getType == 0x02BE) branch that checks deltas and may call AccumulateDexterity(1) on a bull's-eye.
open_questions: Exact format of the suspend result assigned to local1 in U8P_DART::use(): is it a direct item reference, a coordinate tuple, or a menu choice that resolves to an item? (inferred but not explicit).
Precise role of U8P_DART::func046A: appears to drive short actor method spawns/animation timing but internal actor argument layout and side effects are only inferred.

Meaning of some low-confidence local reuses (e.g., local1 used both as captured suspend value and later as Z coordinate) — whether locals are item handles or scalar coordinates in every case remains partially inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_DART::use
main_function.purpose: Handle player U8P_DART::use of the dart: capture/receive a chosen target, validate and animate a throw/placement, compute dexterity-based inaccuracy, spawn timing/effects, and call Item::shoot to launch the dart projectile.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the dart item is used by the player (process type 0x0207).
Suspends to receive a target/selection (suspend assigns into local1).

function_map.name: U8P_DART::use
function_map.role: Orchestrates the dart throw lifecycle: target capture, placement validation, animations, inaccuracy handling, and projectile launch.

function_map.name: U8P_DART::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler that barks the short name 'dart'.

function_map.name: U8P_DART::hit
function_map.role: React to the dart being struck or applying an effect on a U8P_DART::hit target/NPC: play SFX, clear refs, produce barks, handle special-type bull's-eye logic and call AccumulateDexterity.

function_map.name: U8P_DART::func046A
function_map.role: Spawn and wait for two short actor method calls (likely driving throw-related animations/timing); used by U8P_DART::use() during the throw animation sequence.

internal_calls.purpose: Run short actor method spawns / timing steps as part of the pre-throw animation sequence and wait for them to complete.

external_calls.purpose: Read X coordinate of target/item for aiming and placement computation.
Read Y coordinate of target/item for aiming and placement computation.
Read Z/elevation/frame for target and thrower for height offsets and shoot origin.
Compute direction/index from thrower to target coordinates (used to pick placement offsets and animation frames).
Validate whether a candidate placement location (x,y,dir) is legal for the item before performing push/pop and shoot.
Confirm or reserve a legal move/placement; its out param (local7) gates throw continuation.
Spawn a short animation/method on the item/NPC (pre-throw animation) and suspend until it yields/completes.
Set the item's frame/visual state according to computed direction/state after the pre-throw spawn.
Temporarily add/publish the item to world state to begin timed pre-shoot lifecycle.
Spawn a free routine used as a timed delay/cleanup step after push; U8P_DART::use() waits on it to time the sequence.
Remove/unreserve the pushed item from the world after the delay/animation completes.
Spawn an effect or sound routine near the pop/shoot event (SFX/effect).
Read the thrower's dexterity to compute accuracy/inaccuracy magnitude.
Generate random offsets used to model throw inaccuracy based on computed range.
Launch the dart projectile using the computed coordinates and item context (final action of U8P_DART::use()).
Test whether the U8P_DART::hit target (param1) is an NPC so NPC reaction logic runs.
Check whether the targeted NPC is dead; suppress living-NPC reactions if dead.
Play impact/feedback sound effects on hits and special-type events.
Clear stored references/registrations for the item/target as part of U8P_DART::hit cleanup.
Pick a random variant for NPC barks on U8P_DART::hit.
Trigger the U8P_DART::hit NPC's enterFastArea process after being U8P_DART::hit (spawn and wait).
Detect a special item type (0x02BE) to run the bull's-eye branch.
Set a status bit (0x1000) on the item in the special-type branch.
Compute Y delta between target and this item for proximity/orientation bull's-eye checks.
Compute Z/elevation delta for bull's-eye condition.
Award or record a dexterity gain when a bull's-eye condition is met.
Spawn short METHOD::func15E7 runs twice (different sub-arguments) on the actor and wait for each to complete (likely animation/timing).
Compute the direction argument to pass to each METHOD::func15E7 spawn.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements the gameplay for a thrown dart item: selecting/receiving a target, validating a candidate placement/throw origin, running short pre-throw animations, pushing the dart into the world for timing, applying dexterity-based inaccuracy, and finally launching the projectile via Item::shoot.
